2013-01-01.title: Israel: Ex-Soldier Detained in Egypt
2013-01-01.abstract: A former Israeli soldier known for his pro-Palestinian activism and radical views, was detained in Egypt, apparently having entered the country without travel documents.

2013-01-01.title: Is China Retaliating Against Western Journalists?
2013-01-01.abstract: Many companies, including technology giants like Google, have faced the challenge of how to work in China without getting on the wrong side of Chinese authorities.

2013-01-01.title: Rangers in Isolated Central Africa Uncover Grim Cost of Protecting Wildlife
2013-01-01.abstract: Increasingly, the rangers, many in their 40s or 50s, are finding themselves wading into the bush to confront hardened soldiers working as ivory poachers.

2013-01-01.title: 1991 Victory Over Iraq Was Swift, but Hardly Flawless
2013-01-01.abstract: The 1991 Persian Gulf war has been cast as a model for a successful intervention, but debates continue today about the way the White House brought the war to an abrupt end.

2013-01-01.title: Trendy Spot Urges Tourists to Ride In and Spend, ‘Gangnam Style’
2013-01-01.abstract: The success of the “Gangnam Style” video, by a midlevel star who goes by Psy, has fueled grand ambitions by a district that wants to be known as a center of conspicuous consumption.

2013-01-02.title: Syria: Government Pounds Damascus Suburbs
2013-01-02.abstract: Government forces continued to pound the Damascus suburbs with artillery and airstrikes on Tuesday.

2013-01-02.title: South Africa: Fires Rip Through Cape Town Slum
2013-01-02.abstract: At least three people died and thousands were left homeless after several fast-moving fires swept through a crowded slum at the edge of Cape Town on Tuesday.

2013-01-02.title: Egypt: Prosecutor Opens Criminal Investigation Against Comedian Accused of Insulting the President
2013-01-02.abstract: Prosecutors in Cairo opened a criminal investigation on Tuesday into allegations that a popular television comedian, Bassem Youssef, has taken special aim at ultraconservative Islamists on his program.

2013-01-02.title: Sudan and South Sudan Leaders Agree to Meet
2013-01-02.abstract: President Omar al-Bashir of Sudan has agreed to meet with President Salva Kiir Miyardeit of South Sudan on Friday in Ethiopia to discuss border security and oil sharing.

2013-01-02.title: Studying Marijuana and Its Loftier Purpose
2013-01-02.abstract: Tikkun Olam, a government-approved farm where new strains of cannabis are being developed, is at the center of the debate in Israel on the legality, benefits and risks of medical marijuana.

2013-01-03.title: Plane Crash? Murders? Time to Play Thai Lottery
2013-01-03.abstract: Thais, who often mix superstition with a proclivity for fun, play the underground lottery with numbers gleaned from calamity, believing that disaster can beget good fortune.

2013-01-03.title: Central Africa on the Brink, Rebels Halt Their Advance
2013-01-03.abstract: A new willingness to talk raises hope of a peaceful resolution to a conflict that has driven thousands of civilians from their homes in search of refuge.

2013-01-03.title: Iraq: Maliki Demands That Protesters Stand Down
2013-01-03.abstract: Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki told demonstrators on Wednesday to stop their protests or face government action.

2013-01-03.title: Image of the Day: Jan. 2
2013-01-03.abstract: Slum dwellers demand better housing for urban poor in Mumbai.

2013-01-03.title: Afghan War Commander Gives Options for After ’14
2013-01-03.abstract: Gen. John R. Allen has submitted military options to the Pentagon that would keep 6,000 to 20,000 American troops in Afghanistan after the NATO mission ends in 2014.

2013-01-04.title: A Survivor of College Rejection Refuses to Lose Her Resolve
2013-01-04.abstract: Sush Krishnamoorthy, a student in New Delhi who is applying to universities in the United States, is looking forward to an exciting life, “regardless of where I go to college.”

2013-01-04.title: France: Basque Party to Disband
2013-01-04.abstract: The Basque nationalist political party, Batasuna, already illegal in Spain, announced Thursday that it would dissolve itself in France as well.

2013-01-04.title: An Afghan Soldier’s Journey From Ally to Enemy of America

2013-01-04.title: Prostitutes in Israel Are Finding New Lives in Training for the Fashion World
2013-01-04.abstract: Free courses in styling, the retail clothing business, dress design and sewing give women self confidence and job prospects.

2013-01-04.title: 2 Americans Are Advised Not to Visit North Korea
2013-01-04.abstract: Former Gov. Bill Richardson of New Mexico and Google’s executive chairman, Eric E. Schmidt, were advised against a visit to the country, though an expert says it might aid in the discussion of a detained American citizen.

2013-01-05.title: Debit and Credit Card Purchases Shut Down at Vatican
2013-01-05.abstract: The move follows a Bank of Italy decision based on concerns over financial accountability and money-laundering controls.

2013-01-05.title: Fleeing North Korea Is Becoming Harder
2013-01-05.abstract: The tightening of controls at the border led to a fall of about 44 percent decrease over the previous year in the number of North Koreans defecting to South Korea in 2012.

2013-01-05.abstract: Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki has come under fire for measures that some lawmakers and political opponents see as an attempt to monopolize power ahead of provincial elections.

2013-01-05.abstract: The authorities in São Paulo, Brazil’s most populous state, said they would start detaining addicts of crack cocaine and sending them against their will to treatment centers.

2013-01-05.title: Church of England Ends Ban on Gay Men as Bishops
2013-01-05.abstract: The Church of England said Friday that gay clergymen in civil partnerships could become bishops as long as they vowed to remain celibate.

2013-01-06.title: Leaders Plan Buffer Zone at Sudan Border
2013-01-06.abstract: Presidents Omar Hassan al-Bashir of Sudan and Salva Kiir of South Sudan had agreed to pull back their armies before, but have yet to do so.

2013-01-06.title: U.S. Weighs Fewer Troops After 2014 in Afghanistan
2013-01-06.abstract: The Obama administration is considering a range of as low as 3,000 troops and as high of 9,000.

2013-01-06.title: As Putin’s Grip Gets Tighter, a Time of Protest Fades in Russia
2013-01-06.abstract: The excitement around last year’s street activism against Vladimir V. Putin, now president again, is gone. But steps by the government are still setting off waves of indignation.

2013-01-06.title: As Biofuel Demand Grows, So Do Guatemala’s Hunger Pangs
2013-01-06.abstract: Biofuel expansion has contributed to spikes in food prices and a shortage of farmland; both are felt keenly in Guatemala, with its corn-based diet and land owned by a handful of families.

2013-01-06.abstract: The expansion of biofuels has contributed to spikes in food prices and a shortage of land for agriculture in poor corners of Asia, Africa and Latin America. Nowhere, perhaps, is that squeeze more obvious than in Guatemala.

2013-01-07.title: Hundreds in Peru Balk at Relocation From Site of Mine

2013-01-07.abstract: A new law in Russia restricts beer sales at train stations and sidewalk kiosks, but some are uncertain that overall alcohol consumption will decrease.

2013-01-07.title: Annual Buildup of Air Pollution Chokes Tehran
2013-01-07.abstract: The haze occurs every year when cold air and windless days trap fumes belched out by millions of cars and hundreds of old factories.

2013-01-07.title: Relocation in the Andes
2013-01-07.abstract: Perched in the Peruvian Andes is a new town built by a Chinese mining company to which 5,000 people will be relocated.

2013-01-07.title: India’s Rape and Sexual Assault Laws Under Scrutiny
2013-01-07.abstract: Outdated concepts like “outraging the modesty of a woman” and unclear guidelines about punishment need to be changed, activists say.

2013-01-08.abstract: The oil minister, Rostam Qasemi, acknowledged that petroleum exports and sales had fallen by at least 40 percent over the past year, contradicting his previous denials.

2013-01-08.abstract: In a show of international cooperation that involved Russia and Iraq, Syria was persuaded to stop chemical bomb preparation. But the United States and Europe are not resting easy.

2013-01-08.abstract: Ban Ki-moon, the United Nations secretary general, expressed dismay over a defiant speech on Sunday by President Bashar al-Assad of Syria.

2013-01-08.abstract: The patriarch of the Russian Orthodox Church encouraged Russians to adopt orphans, as the country adjusts to a decision to bar Americans from adopting Russian children.

2013-01-08.abstract: Detective Chief Inspector April Casburn is accused of seeking to sell confidential police information to a British tabloid owned by Rupert Murdoch.

2013-01-09.abstract: Officials in the Sakha-Yakutia region of Siberia have gone so far as to declare a state of emergency to draw attention to the wolves eating livestock, including horses and reindeer.

2013-01-09.abstract: Jérôme Cahuzac, who is leading a crackdown on tax fraud, was told Tuesday that he would face a preliminary investigation into charges that he kept an illegal bank account abroad.

2013-01-09.abstract: Peace has eased safety concerns in Helmand Province, but there is little faith in the Afghan government’s ability to maintain the security gains won by American and British troops.

2013-01-09.abstract: Gen. Stanley A. McChrystal said the United States still needs to keep forces in Afghanistan to help stabilize the country.

2013-01-10.abstract: Houses with secluded gardens and courtyards were widely demolished to make way for glassed apartment blocks, but some Iranians are beginning to reverse the trend.

2013-01-10.abstract: President Hugo Chávez’s health crisis and decision to proceed on Thursday with a quasi-presidential inauguration that he is unable to attend is producing national angst about who is in charge.

2013-01-11.title: For International Students, College Checklists for January

2013-01-12.abstract: Kiki Dimoula’s poetry — spare, profound, unsentimental, drawing on the powerful themes of time, fate and destiny — has earned her a near-cult following in Greece.

2013-01-12.abstract: The ban on American adoptions of Russian children began as a dispute between two countries, but Russians are now fighting among themselves over the ban.

2013-01-19.abstract: A trip to Europe for Leon E. Panetta, the United States’ defense secretary, had the feel of a valedictory lap, but the tenor quickly changed with news of abductions in Algeria.

2013-01-19.abstract: A senior Pakistani diplomat said Friday that his government would release all Afghan Taliban prisoners in its custody, bolstering hopes for peace negotiations with the insurgents.

2013-01-20.abstract: Rahul Gandhi was appointed vice president of the ruling National Congress Party, but whether he will serve as the party’s candidate for prime minister next year is still unclear.

Priest Is Planning to
Defy the Vatican’s
Orders to Stay Quiet
2013-01-20.abstract: A priest in Ireland plans to speak on what he says is the Catholic Church’s campaign against him over his advocacy regarding matters like female priests and contraception.

2013-01-21.abstract: China accused Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton of presenting a distorted picture about its dispute with Japan, and it expressed “resolute opposition” to her position.

2013-01-22.abstract: Malian and French troops appeared to recapture two important towns from militants who have overrun the country’s northern half.

2013-01-23.abstract: In many rape cases, the police spend more time seeking reconciliation, even marriage, between the attacker and the victim, instead of an investigation.

2013-01-24.abstract: A French lawyer working with Florence Cassez’s family said she would leave as soon as possible for France.

2013-01-25.abstract: If you are a student interested in taking a variety of university courses and having time to narrow your career options, then a liberal arts education may be ideal for you.

2013-01-25.abstract: As French forces fight Islamist rebels in Mali,  the country has  ramped up police and army presence at home and is sending special forces to help protect uranium production sites in Niger.

2013-01-25.abstract: An American who helped plan the deadly 2008 terrorist attacks in Mumbai provided evidence against others in the plot after he was arrested.

2013-01-26.title: At Davos, Hunting for the Next Big Thing….uh, Country
2013-01-26.abstract: As Brazil and Argentina lose some of their luster, are sub-Saharan African nations on the rise? Billionaire dealmakers want to know.

2013-01-26.title: Somalia: Twitter Shuts Rebels’ Account
2013-01-26.abstract: The Twitter account of the Shabab militant group in Somalia, which used to trumpet the group’s battlefield prowess and once had thousands of followers, has been shut down.

2013-01-26.title: Britain: A Bill for Same-Sex Marriage
2013-01-26.abstract: Prime Minister David Cameron’s government on Friday published a bill to legalize same-sex marriage.

2013-01-26.title: ‘Propaganda’ by Gays Faces Russian Curbs Amid Unrest
2013-01-26.abstract: Police arrested about 20 people as attackers hurled eggs and paint at gay rights advocates who gathered in Moscow to protest a bill that outlaws “homosexual propaganda.”

2013-01-26.title: France: Striking a Blow for the Mother Tongue
2013-01-26.abstract: France has decreed that Twitter-using citizens should refrain from using the word “hashtag” in favor of the newly coined French term “mot-dièse.”

2013-01-27.title: Mexican Violence Prompts Self-Policing by Civilians
2013-01-27.abstract: The vigilante movement in rural southwestern Mexico shines a light on the lack of state security as a new administration prepares to take on the country’s violence.

2013-01-27.title: Leader’s Visit Lifts a Village, Yet Lays Bare China’s Woes
2013-01-27.abstract: Xi Jinping’s visit to a particularly poverty-stricken village in north China brought it attention and help, but also threw into relief the vast scale of the challenge.

2013-01-27.title: A Village in the Spotlight
2013-01-27.abstract: A remote mountain hamlet in China draws attention after a visit from Xi Jinping, the new Communist Party chief.

2013-01-27.title: Mexico: Statue of Ex-Leader of Azerbaijan Removed
2013-01-27.abstract: The Mexico City government removed a large statue of a former president of Azerbaijan from a central boulevard early Saturday, giving in after months of complaints by critics.

2013-01-27.title: North Korean Leader Vows ‘High-Profile’ Retaliation Over New U.N. Sanctions
2013-01-27.abstract: North Korea did not clarify what those measures might be, but referred to earlier threats to launch more long-range rockets and conduct a third nuclear test.

2013-01-28.title: Bangladesh Factory, Site of Fire That Trapped and Killed 7, Made European Brands
2013-01-28.abstract: Labor advocates, who have pressed global brands to help pay for fire-safety measures in Bangladeshi factories, said the latest fire was a grim reminder of how conditions must be improved.

2013-01-28.title: Britain Warns Its Citizens in Somaliland to Leave
2013-01-28.abstract: A person who has been briefed on the British warning said that a terrorist organization, most likely the Shabab, had threatened to kidnap foreigners in Hargeisa, the capital of Somaliland.

2013-01-28.title: Italians Have a New Tool to Unearth Tax Cheats
2013-01-28.abstract: The new tool, known as the “redditometro,” aims to minimize the wiggle room for evasion by examining a taxpayer’s expenditures and comparing them with declared income.

2013-01-28.title: Nguyen Khanh, General Who Led Coup, Dies at 86
2013-01-28.abstract: General Khanh bloodlessly seized control of South Vietnam’s government in 1964, but his rule as premier lasted only a year as he was deposed by a junta of junior officers.

2013-01-28.title: Egypt’s Leader Declares State of Emergency in Three Cities
2013-01-28.abstract: President Mohamed Morsi resorted to the authoritarian weapon of his deposed predecessor as escalating violence in the streets threatened his government and Egypt’s democracy.

2013-01-29.title: Israeli Secularists Appear to Find Their Voice
2013-01-29.abstract: The stunning success of Yair Lapid and his Yesh Atid Party in last week’s election is being viewed as a victory for the secular mainstream in the identity battle gripping Israel.

2013-01-29.title: Chile: Former Leaders of German Sect Sentenced
2013-01-29.abstract: The Supreme Court announced Monday that six former leaders of Colonia Dignidad, a German sect in southern Chile, had been sentenced to 5 to 11 years in prison.

2013-01-29.title: Venezuela: Official Death Toll in Prison Clash Is 58
2013-01-29.abstract: The minister of prisons, Iris Varela, said Monday that a prison clash that broke out Friday between inmates and National Guard soldiers had killed 58 people and injured 95.

2013-01-29.title: The Netherlands: Queen Says She Will Step Down
2013-01-29.abstract: Queen Beatrix announced Monday that she would abdicate on April 30 after serving as the Netherlands’ head of state for 33 years.

2013-01-29.title: France Says Syrian Rebels Need Aid to Fend Off ‘Chaos’
2013-01-29.abstract: At a Paris meeting of the Friends of Syria, Foreign Minister Laurent Fabius warned that without the aid promised, control of Syria could fall to militant Islamists.

2013-01-30.title: After Fire, Some Brazilians Fault a Nation’s Sense of Fatalism
2013-01-30.abstract: The nightclub fire in Santa Maria, Brazil, that killed more than 230 people Sunday has set off a debate about how — or even if — the country can prevent such disasters.

2013-01-30.title: Nigeria: Money Promised to Clean Up Lead That Killed Hundreds of Children
2013-01-30.abstract: President Goodluck Jonathan promised this week to release $4 million to clean up villages where hundreds of children have died in the world’s worst outbreak of lead poisoning.

2013-01-30.title: Welcome to Britain. Our Weather Is Appalling.
2013-01-30.abstract: A new guidebook for immigrants reflects the Conservative view that too many people are trying to move to Britain, and that once they arrive they are failing to appreciate the country properly.

2013-01-30.title: In Thailand, Power Comes With Help From Skype
2013-01-30.abstract: Thaksin Shinawatra, the country’s most famous fugitive and a former prime minister, has harnessed the Internet and mobile technology to create an unusual way of governing.

2013-01-30.title: Gallows Humor, and Smog, Engulf China
2013-01-30.abstract: As Beijing and large parts of the country suffer persistent, heavy smog, a former environment chief blamed chaotic economic growth, rooted in a “rule of men,” not law, for the mess.

2013-01-31.title: Drone Strike Prompts Suit, Raising Fears for U.S. Allies
2013-01-31.abstract: The United States often uses intelligence from its allies to target drone strikes, and now British officials are the subject of a lawsuit over the issue.

2013-01-31.title: Some Fear a Soccer Team’s Racist Fans Hold a Mirror Up to Israel
2013-01-31.abstract: A team owner’s plans to recruit two Muslim players from Chechnya have triggered intense and ugly protests.

2013-01-31.title: Afghans Bristle at U.S. Ban on Airline
2013-01-31.abstract: Allegations of opium smuggling halted military contracts, and some American officials, too, have quietly questioned the response.

2013-01-31.title: Russia Cancels Agreement With U.S.
2013-01-31.abstract: The cancellation of a decade-long agreement that called for cooperation on law enforcement efforts was the latest example of strained relations between the two countries.

2013-01-31.title: Factory Owners Arrested in Bangladesh
2013-01-31.abstract: Sharif Ahmed, chairman of Smart Export Garments, and his colleague, M. D. Zakir Ahmed, were arrested after a fire last week left seven workers dead.
